ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002694 covers generic french fries. Signature seasoned skin-on variant is not individually catalogued; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers fried potato products broadly. Seasoned skin-on variant not individually listed; closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for seasoned fries; not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large serving of Wingstop Signature Seasoned Fries is approximately 200 g, equivalent to a standard fast-food large fries portion.

nutrient_source.title: Potato, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, oven-heated — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172004)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for oven-heated frozen french fries used as the closest defensible analogue for thick-cut skin-on seasoned fries. Sodium adjusted upward by approximately 15% to account for the additional seasoning blend applied at Wingstop, consistent with published seasoned-fries comparisons. All other macros retained from USDA baseline. Values represent a reasonable estimate for a seasoned skin-on deep-fried potato product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 derived from University of Sydney GI Database entry for french fries (GI ~75, glucose reference). Thick-cut skin-on seasoned fries are expected to have a similar or slightly lower GI than thin-cut fries due to lower surface-area-to-volume ratio; GI 75 is used as a conservative upper-bound estimate. GL per serving calculated as (75 × 43.0 × 2.0) / 100 = 64.5 for a 200 g large serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~780 mg per large serving)
hypertension.guidance: A large serving of these seasoned fries provides approximately 780 mg sodium, representing about 34% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or avoid this item.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (75) and high glycemic load (~64.5 per large serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 75 and a glycemic load of approximately 64.5 per large serving, these fries can cause a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and pair with low-GI foods if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, and sodium; deep-fried preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A large serving delivers approximately 30 g total fat, 4.6 g saturated fat, and 780 mg sodium; the deep-fried preparation and seasoning load make this a high-risk item for cardiovascular disease patients who should minimise intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Signature Seasoned Fries (Large) is a fast-food side dish served at Wingstop restaurants in Singapore. The fries are thick-cut and skin-on, made from whole potatoes with the skin retained, then deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. After frying, they are tossed in Wingstop's proprietary seasoning blend, which typically includes salt, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, and other spices, giving the fries a savoury, mildly spiced flavour with a crispy exterior and fluffy interior. The large serving size is approximately 200 g. Nutritionally, a large serving provides around 646 kcal, 86 g carbohydrate, 30 g fat, and 7 g protein, with a high glycemic index of approximately 75 and a glycemic load of about 64.5. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 780 mg per serving due to the seasoning blend. These fries are halal-certified at Wingstop Singapore and are suitable for vegetarians and vegans. They are not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to the high GI, high sodium, and high fat content. The skin-on preparation retains more dietary fibre and potassium compared to peeled fries. Wingstop is an American fried chicken chain with outlets at City Square Mall and Funan in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Wingstop large fries?
What is the GI of seasoned fries?
Are Wingstop fries suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for frozen oven-heated french fries (FDC ID 172004), which is the closest approved-source analogue. The exact Wingstop seasoning formulation is proprietary and not published in any approved source; sodium has been adjusted upward to account for seasoning. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for french fries. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match for this branded seasoned product exists in any approved primary source.
notes: Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this fried potato product in the USDA SR Legacy source used. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. Vitamin B12 (vitamin_b12_mcg) is 0.0 as this is a plant-based fried potato product containing no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for potato-based products. Cholesterol (cholesterol_mg) is 0.0 as this product is prepared in vegetable oil with no animal-derived fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for vegetable-oil-fried potato products. Sodium value of 390 mg per 100 g is an upward adaptation from the USDA baseline (~340 mg/100 g) to account for the additional seasoning blend. Halal status confirmed from field survey data cuisine tags and Wingstop Singapore's publicly stated halal certification. Serving size of 200 g is an estimate for a large fast-food fries portion consistent with published fast-food portion data; exact Wingstop large serving weight is not published in an approved source.
